title: Resolve merge conflicts for Fix remaining 'orchestrator' references in CLI output and onboarding
description:
Merge conflict detected during preflight merge of main into feature/b-aa3e7707.
Conflicted files: src/agent_takt/cli.py, src/agent_takt/onboarding.py

Git error: Auto-merging docs/onboarding.md
Auto-merging src/agent_takt/cli.py
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in src/agent_takt/cli.py
Auto-merging src/agent_takt/onboarding.py
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in src/agent_takt/onboarding.py
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.

Diff context:
diff --cc src/agent_takt/cli.py
index ee69490,a37fda6..0000000
--- a/src/agent_takt/cli.py
+++ b/src/agent_takt/cli.py
@@@ -135,6 -136,7 +136,10 @@@ def apply_operator_status_update(storag
  
  def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
      parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="takt")
++<<<<<<< HEAD
++=======
+     parser.add_argument("--version", action="version", version=f"takt {_pkg_version('agent-takt')}")
++>>>>>>> main
      parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Repository root")
      parser.add_argument(
          "--runner",
diff --cc src/agent_takt/onboarding.py
index 8438e63,5b68cb2..0000000
--- a/src/agent_takt/onboarding.py
+++ b/src/agent_takt/onboarding.py
@@@ -346,7 -347,6 +347,10 @@@ def collect_init_answers
      out = stream_out or sys.stdout
      inp = stream_in or sys.stdin
  
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +    out.write("=== takt init ===\n")
++=======
++>>>>>>> main
      out.write("Press Enter to accept the default shown in [brackets].\n\n")
      out.flush()
  
@@@ -697,18 -699,20 +703,29 @@@ def scaffold_project
  
      # 6. Update .gitignore
      if update_gitignore(project_root):
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +        _log("Updated .gitignore with takt entries")
++=======
+         console.success("Updated .gitignore with orchestrator entries")
++>>>>>>> main
      else:
-         _log("Skipped .gitignore (entries already present)")
+         console.warn("Skipped .gitignore (entries already present)")
  
      # 7. Create specs/ structure and HOWTO
      (project_root / "specs" / "done").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
      (project_root / "specs" / "drafts").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
      howto = create_specs_howto(project_root, overwrite=overwrite)
      if howto:
-         _log("Created specs/HOWTO.md")
+         console.success("Created specs/HOWTO.md")
      else:
-         _log("Skipped specs/HOWTO.md (already exists)")
+         console.warn("Skipped specs/HOWTO.md (already exists)")
  
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +    out.write("\nDone. Run `takt summary` to verify the setup.\n")
 +    out.flush()
++=======
+     console.emit(
+         f"\n{console._c(BOLD)}{console._c(GREEN)}Done.{console._c(RESET)}"
+         " Run `takt summary` to verify the setup."
+     )
++>>>>>>> main

handoff_summary.completed: Applied all structural additions from main (--version flag, _pkg_version import, subparser help strings, console.section init header, ConsoleReporter scaffold_project refactor, TTY-aware _c() in ConsoleReporter, spec-management skill files) while rejecting main's "orchestrator" regressions. Added test_cli_version.py, TestConsoleReporterColorBehavior, TestCommandInitSectionHeader, TestInstallAgentsSkillsSpecManagement, TestScaffoldProjectSpecManagement. Committed as 78dd80a.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. The merge conflict bead is complete. The feature branch now incorporates all structural improvements from main with correct takt branding. The takt merge command can be retried.
handoff_summary.risks: The test_console.py TestConsoleReporterColorBehavior.test_scaffold_done_line_no_ansi_on_non_tty test uses patch for packaged asset dirs — if the packaged_agents_skills_dir and packaged_claude_skills_dir patch targets differ from actual import paths, the test may not intercept correctly. The tester agent should verify this.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run takt merge B-aa3e7707 to retry the feature merge now that conflicts are resolved.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Resolved. No remaining file-scope conflicts expected. The scheduler.py and tui.py files were intentionally NOT changed — the feature branch's "takt" versions are correct; main's regressions to "orchestrator" were rejected.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Took all structural improvements from main (ConsoleReporter refactor, --version flag, TTY-aware ANSI, spec-management skill) but rejected main's "orchestrator" regressions. The _c() method pattern (return code if TTY else empty string) cleanly separates TTY detection from ANSI emission. The scaffold_project console parameter (with stream_out as fallback) preserves backward compatibility for callers that pass stream_out directly.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: New tests cover: --version exits 0, version string format matches installed package, prog name is "takt", help usage references "takt" not "orchestrator", all subcommands still parse. ConsoleReporter color behavior: _c() returns code on TTY/empty on non-TTY, all emit methods suppress ANSI on non-TTY. command_init section header appears before scaffold. spec-management: SKILL.md/spec.py/openai.yaml are installed, spec.py init/list/create subcommands work. scaffold_project end-to-end installs spec-management with both runners.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The test_scaffold_done_line_no_ansi_on_non_tty test patches packaged_agents_skills_dir and packaged_claude_skills_dir — these patches may not fully intercept actual directory resolution in all code paths if install_agents_skills/install_claude_skills bypass the patched values. The tester should verify these tests pass.

conflict_risks: Resolved. No remaining file-scope conflicts expected. The scheduler.py and tui.py files were intentionally NOT changed — the feature branch's "takt" versions are correct; main's regressions to "orchestrator" were rejected.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-c3e4d654-test
Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-c3e4d654-docs

Resolved merge conflicts between feature/b-aa3e7707 and main by integrating structural improvements from main while preserving the feature branch's "takt" branding throughout. Applied 9 file changes: --version flag and subparser help strings in cli.py, ConsoleReporter refactor in onboarding.py (scaffold_project now uses ConsoleReporter with TTY-aware ANSI), _c() guard method in console.py, new spec-management task skill data files, and corresponding new/extended tests.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
